objective: Use imported BOQ files, rate-bearing workbooks, and quantity structures from the corpus as the historical foundation for BOQ generation in UAE workflows.
boqOutputRequirements: Separate preliminaries, substructure, superstructure, architectural finishes, MEP, external works, and summary sections.
Preserve quantity, unit, rate, amount, exclusions, inclusions, and commercial clarification structure.
Prefer bilingual-ready descriptions when the project requires Arabic/English output.
Treat historical BOQ and rate files as pricing foundations, not as blind final rates.
trainingRules: Historical BOQ files and project rates are a pricing foundation and formatting memory, not a final authority.
Prefer BOQ structures that repeat consistently across project type and location clusters.
Keep quantities linked to the latest QTO and flag mismatches between corpus patterns and live project evidence.
Combine historical internal price memory with live public-web research before proposing final unit rates.
